title: Zhuangzi, Chapter 16: Mending the Inborn Nature
description: Chapter 16 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.
introduction: Chapter 16 of the Zhuangzi — the Taoist masterpiece of paradox and parable.

translation.text: Those who would mend their inborn nature with vulgar learning, hoping by this to return to its original condition; those who would bring order to their desires through vulgar thoughts, hoping by this to attain clarity — they may be called blind and deluded people.
